# opencenter

> opencenter is a configuration-first CLI for Kubernetes cluster configuration, GitOps repository generation, deployment, validation, and SOPS-backed secret workflows.

## Current GA Command Surface

Use this file as a compact command reference for language-model consumers. The generated Cobra reference in `docs/reference/opencenter/` is the source of truth for exact flags.

### Global Flags

These root flags are available where they make sense:

```bash
opencenter --config-dir ~/.config/opencenter --log-level info --output json cluster list
opencenter --quiet cluster active
opencenter --dry-run cluster generate my-cluster
opencenter --yes cluster destroy my-cluster
```

Supported output formats are `text`, `json`, and `yaml`. Structured output is intended for commands that return inspectable data, such as listing, status, validation, and secrets inspection.

## Cluster Lifecycle

Create a cluster configuration:

```bash
opencenter cluster init my-cluster
opencenter cluster init my-cluster --org myorg
opencenter cluster init vmware-cluster --type vmware --org production
opencenter cluster init dev-cluster --type kind
```

Open the guided provider configuration workflow:

```bash
opencenter cluster configure myorg/my-cluster --guided
```

Validate configuration:

```bash
opencenter cluster validate myorg/my-cluster
opencenter cluster validate myorg/my-cluster --manifests
opencenter cluster validate myorg/my-cluster --output json
```

Generate GitOps assets:

```bash
opencenter cluster generate myorg/my-cluster
opencenter cluster generate myorg/my-cluster --render-only
```

Deploy or restart deployment:

```bash
opencenter cluster deploy myorg/my-cluster
opencenter cluster deploy myorg/my-cluster --restart
```

Inspect lifecycle state:

```bash
opencenter cluster status myorg/my-cluster
opencenter cluster status myorg/my-cluster --paths
opencenter cluster status myorg/my-cluster --output json
opencenter cluster status myorg/my-cluster --sync
```

## Active Cluster Context

Use an explicit `org/name` when possible. For repeated work, set and inspect the active cluster:

```bash
opencenter cluster use myorg/my-cluster
opencenter cluster active
opencenter cluster list
eval "$(opencenter cluster env)"
```

If a command reports that no active cluster is set, either run `opencenter cluster use <org/name>` or pass `<org/name>` to the command that failed.

## Configuration Management

Patch one or more fields with dot-path assignments:

```bash
opencenter cluster set myorg/my-cluster opencenter.meta.env=staging
opencenter cluster set myorg/my-cluster opencenter.gitops.repository.url=https://github.com/acme/platform.git
```

Normalize and export effective configuration:

```bash
opencenter cluster normalize myorg/my-cluster
opencenter cluster export myorg/my-cluster --output-file effective-config.yaml
```

The per-cluster YAML remains the source of truth. Generated assets should be treated as outputs of `cluster generate`.

## Secrets

Secret operations are grouped under `secrets`. Key lifecycle commands are nested under `secrets keys`:

```bash
opencenter secrets list
opencenter secrets validate myorg/my-cluster
opencenter secrets encrypt myorg/my-cluster
opencenter secrets decrypt myorg/my-cluster
opencenter secrets keys generate
opencenter secrets keys list
opencenter secrets keys rotate myorg/my-cluster --type sops
opencenter secrets keys backup
```

SOPS Age encryption is configured during cluster initialization. Keep private keys and token files outside Git.

## Providers

Supported infrastructure providers include OpenStack, VMware, Kind, AWS, GCP, Azure, and bare metal depending on the configured workflow.

OpenStack and Kind workflows support generation and deployment through the CLI. VMware workflows expect pre-provisioned VMs and provider-specific configuration before generation.

## GitOps

Cluster generation creates or updates the local GitOps workspace configured in:

```yaml
opencenter:
  gitops:
    repository:
      url: https://github.com/acme/platform.git
      branch: main
      local_dir: ./gitops
```

Run `cluster generate` after configuration changes, then `cluster deploy` when ready to apply the lifecycle workflow.

## Troubleshooting

Common checks:

```bash
opencenter cluster validate myorg/my-cluster
opencenter cluster status myorg/my-cluster --paths
opencenter cluster doctor myorg/my-cluster
opencenter secrets validate myorg/my-cluster
```

Use `--output json` or `--output yaml` on supported read-only commands when automation needs structured data.
